expectations.text: Correctly identifies the user as a higher rate (40%) taxpayer
expectations.evidence: Line 22: 'At £72,000 you are a higher-rate taxpayer' and explicit 40% rate shown in income tax table (line 28)

expectations.text: Mentions the CGT annual exempt amount (£3,000 for 2024/25)
Explains the mid-year CGT rate change on 30 October 2024 or different rates before/after that date
expectations.evidence: Lines 75-78: Clear explanation that 'The CGT rate on crypto (a non-residential-property asset) changed on 30 October 2024' with before (20%) and after (24%) rates for higher-rate taxpayers

expectations.text: Explains SIP 3-5 year holding period means partial income tax and NI liability on shares
expectations.evidence: Line 124: Table row '3 to 5 years | Lower of: (a) market value at award OR (b) market value at withdrawal — whichever is lower, is taxed' with detailed explanation in following paragraphs

expectations.text: States Plan 2 student loan repayment threshold (£27,295) and rate (9%)
expectations.evidence: Lines 164-165: 'For 2024/25, Plan 2 repayments are calculated as: 9% of income above £27,295'

expectations.text: Calculates or explains the student loan repayment amount
expectations.evidence: Lines 169-174: Complete calculation table showing Gross income (£72,000), threshold (£27,295), income above threshold (£44,705), and repayment calculation (9% = £4,023)
Line 188: 'Yes — you must file a Self Assessment return for 2024/25' with clear trigger explanation about CGT gain exceeding AEA

expectations.text: Includes a disclaimer that this is not professional tax advice
expectations.evidence: Lines 1-3: 'DISCLAIMER: This guidance provides general tax information only. It is NOT professional tax advice.'
